note: Domain Source is a submodule of Domain (light-documented): assigns content a canonical source domain and rewrites its outbound URLs to it. These are recipe (easy) cases only, graded on response text; no site changes.

evals.prompt: What does the Domain Source module do to a node's outbound URLs, and which field on the node controls it?
evals.expected_output: It rewrites the node's outbound URLs to point at its canonical 'source' domain instead of the currently active domain. The field_domain_source field (single-value entity-reference to a domain) sets that source domain.

evals.prompt: Why would a multi-domain Drupal site (using Domain Access) also enable Domain Source? What problem does it solve?
evals.expected_output: When one node is shared across several affiliate domains, Domain Source gives it a single canonical URL (its source domain) so links, feeds, and shares point at one authoritative host, avoiding duplicate-content/SEO problems.

evals.prompt: In Domain Source, how can you stop certain routes from having their URLs rewritten to the source domain? Name the config object / setting and an alter hook.
evals.expected_output: Set exclude_routes in the domain_source.settings config (form at /admin/config/domain/domain_source), or alter it in code with hook_domain_source_excluded_route_names_alter / hook_domain_source_excluded_paths_alter.
